title: hopea subpeltata
intro: Hopea subpeltata, also known as the velvet tree, is a species of tree in the family Dipterocarpaceae, native to Southeast Asia. The medicinal properties of Hopea subpeltata lie in its bark, which has been used in traditional medicine to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions. The health benefits of Hopea subpeltata include its anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties, which can help to reduce inflammation, prevent infections, and promote wound healing. Hopea subpeltata can be prepared into herbal remedies such as infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be used to treat a range of health issues, including digestive problems and respiratory infections.
